title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Configure the Python virtual environment with all required packages for LLVM bitcode processing, ML training, and benchmark execution. Install LLVM toolchain for generating bitcode from C benchmarks.

steps.step2: Install Python dependencies: pip install xgboost==2.0.3 scikit-learn==1.4.0 numpy==1.26.0 pandas==2.1.4 matplotlib==3.8.2 seaborn==0.13.0 llvmlite==0.41.1
steps.step3: Verify LLVM installation: clang --version (should be 14+ for bitcode support)
steps.step4: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data/{raw,processed,results} models figures benchmarks
steps.step5: Set random seed environment variable: export EXPERIMENT_SEED=42

title: Download and Prepare Benchmark Suite
description: Acquire PolyBench benchmark suite and prepare synthetic microbenchmarks. These benchmarks will provide diverse structure access patterns for feature extraction and model evaluation.

steps.step2: Select 15 representative kernels with structure usage: gemm, syrk, syr2k, gemver, gesummv, 2mm, 3mm, doitgen, cholesky, lu, fdtd-2d, jacobi-2d, seidel-2d, heat-3d, nussinov
steps.step3: Create synthetic microbenchmarks with known optimal layouts in benchmarks/synthetic/: (a) hot/cold field separation, (b) array-of-structs vs struct-of-arrays, (c) linked list traversal patterns, (d) tree node access patterns
steps.step4: For each benchmark, create 3 data size variants (SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE) to test scalability
steps.step5: Compile all benchmarks to LLVM bitcode (.bc) using: clang -O0 -emit-llvm -c -o output.bc input.c
steps.step6: Generate ground truth labels by manually analyzing each benchmark's optimal layout (based on known access patterns)
steps.step7: Split benchmarks into train (70%, 14 benchmarks) and test (30%, 6 benchmarks) sets with stratification by benchmark type

title: Extract Static Features from LLVM IR
description: Implement feature extraction pipeline using llvmlite to parse LLVM bitcode and extract static features representing structure access patterns, loop characteristics, and context information.

steps.step1: Implement structural feature extraction: number of fields, field sizes, total struct size, field type categories (pointer/primitive/nested), alignment requirements
steps.step2: Implement access pattern feature extraction: loop nesting depth at access sites, number of access sites per field, control flow dominance relationships, pointer arithmetic patterns, field access co-occurrence within loops
steps.step3: Implement context feature extraction: estimated function hotness (using Wu & Larus-style static heuristics), memory allocation patterns, loop trip count estimates where available
steps.step4: Process all 20 benchmarks and extract features for each struct definition, storing as CSV with columns: benchmark_name, struct_id, field_id, feature_1...feature_N, optimal_layout_label
steps.step5: Generate feature statistics: count total features extracted, report missing values, compute feature correlation matrix
steps.step6: Save processed features to data/processed/features.csv and data/processed/labels.csv

title: Implement and Evaluate Static Heuristics Baseline
description: Implement simple static heuristics adapted from Ball & Larus for data layout decisions. This serves as the 'simple' baseline representing traditional compiler approaches without ML.

steps.step1: Implement heuristic: 'Fields accessed in deeply nested loops are hot' - score = max_loop_nesting_depth
steps.step2: Implement heuristic: 'Fields with frequent accesses are hot' - score = number_of_access_sites
steps.step3: Implement heuristic: 'Fields accessed together should be grouped' - compute field co-occurrence score
steps.step4: Combine heuristics using weighted voting with equal weights (0.33, 0.33, 0.34)
steps.step5: Run predictions on test set for 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) - seeds affect tie-breaking
steps.step6: Evaluate prediction accuracy: precision, recall, F1-score for profitable layout identification
steps.step7: Record results to data/results/baseline_heuristics.csv with columns: seed, precision, recall, f1_score, accuracy

title: Implement and Evaluate Profile-Guided Upper Bound
description: Simulate profile-guided optimization by using the ground truth labels as 'perfect' predictions. This establishes an upper bound on achievable accuracy with perfect runtime information.

steps.step1: Use ground truth labels as predictions (simulating perfect profile information)
steps.step2: Evaluate on test set - this represents 100% accuracy achievable with perfect profiling
steps.step3: Calculate metrics: precision=1.0, recall=1.0, f1_score=1.0 (by definition, using perfect labels)
steps.step4: Use this as reference for computing 'accuracy within X% of profile-guided' metric

title: Train LayoutLearner XGBoost Model
description: Train the main XGBoost model for predicting profitable layout transformations. Use 5-fold cross-validation on training set, then evaluate on held-out test set.

steps.step1: Load training data: data/processed/features.csv and data/processed/labels.csv
steps.step3: Train model with 5-fold cross-validation on training set, tracking AUC and accuracy per fold
steps.step4: Perform hyperparameter tuning with grid search: max_depth in [4, 6, 8], learning_rate in [0.05, 0.1, 0.2]
steps.step5: Select best model based on cross-validation AUC
steps.step6: Train final model on full training set with best hyperparameters
steps.step8: Record training time and model size

title: Evaluate LayoutLearner on Test Set
description: Evaluate the trained LayoutLearner model on the held-out test benchmarks for 3 random seeds to measure prediction accuracy and consistency.

steps.step1: Set random seed: 42, load model from models/layout_learner_xgboost.json
steps.step2: Generate predictions on test set, compute: precision, recall, F1-score, ROC-AUC, accuracy
steps.step4: Compute mean and standard deviation across 3 seeds for each metric
steps.step5: Calculate 'accuracy within 20% of profile-guided' metric: (LayoutLearner_F1 / Profile_Guided_F1) >= 0.8
steps.step6: Per-benchmark analysis: identify which benchmarks were predicted correctly vs incorrectly
steps.step7: Save results to data/results/layoutlearner_main_results.json with structure: {seeds: [{seed: 42, precision: X, recall: Y, ...}], mean: {...}, std: {...}}

title: Ablation Study: Feature Categories
description: Conduct ablation study to measure contribution of each feature category (structural, access pattern, context). Remove one category at a time and measure performance drop.

steps.step1: Ablation 1: Train model without structural features (field counts, sizes, types) - keep access + context
steps.step2: Ablation 2: Train model without access pattern features (loop depth, access sites) - keep structural + context
steps.step3: Ablation 3: Train model without context features (estimated hotness) - keep structural + access
steps.step4: For each ablation, train with same hyperparameters as main experiment, evaluate on test set with 3 seeds
steps.step5: Compute performance drop relative to full model for each ablation
steps.step6: Identify which feature category contributes most to prediction accuracy
steps.step7: Save ablation results to data/results/ablation_features.csv with columns: ablation_type, mean_precision, mean_recall, mean_f1, performance_drop_pct

title: Ablation Study: Model Complexity
description: Study the effect of model complexity on performance and inference time. Test simpler (decision tree) and more complex (deeper XGBoost) variants.

steps.step1: Simple baseline: Single Decision Tree (max_depth=10, no ensemble)
steps.step2: Medium complexity: XGBoost with n_estimators=50, max_depth=4
steps.step3: High complexity: XGBoost with n_estimators=200, max_depth=10
steps.step4: For each variant, train on full training set, evaluate on test set with 3 seeds
steps.step5: Measure inference time per prediction (microseconds) for each model
steps.step6: Plot accuracy vs inference time trade-off curve

title: Cross-Benchmark Generalization Analysis
description: Test model generalization across different benchmark types by training on one category and testing on another.

steps.step1: Train on PolyBench only, test on synthetic benchmarks
steps.step2: Train on synthetic only, test on PolyBench
steps.step3: Compare generalization performance to mixed training (main experiment)
steps.step4: Identify which benchmark types are hardest to generalize to

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform statistical tests to validate that LayoutLearner improvements over baselines are significant and not due to random chance.

steps.step1: Collect per-seed results for LayoutLearner (3 seeds), Static Heuristics (3 seeds)
steps.step2: Perform paired t-test comparing LayoutLearner vs Static Heuristics F1-scores
steps.step3: Compute 95% confidence intervals for mean performance difference
steps.step4: Check if LayoutLearner achieves >2% improvement over -O3 equivalent (simulated by comparing to random baseline)
steps.step5: Record p-values and effect sizes (Cohen's d)
steps.step6: Save statistical test results to data/results/statistical_tests.json

title: Compilation-Time Overhead Analysis
description: Measure the overhead of feature extraction and model inference to verify it remains below 5% threshold.

steps.step1: Measure baseline compilation time: parse LLVM bitcode without feature extraction (10 runs, average)
steps.step2: Measure feature extraction time for each benchmark (10 runs, average)
steps.step3: Measure model inference time: time for XGBoost to predict on extracted features (1000 predictions, average)
steps.step6: Save timing results to data/results/compilation_overhead.csv with columns: benchmark, baseline_time_ms, feature_time_ms, inference_time_ms, total_overhead_pct

title: Success Criteria Validation
description: Validate hypothesis against predefined success criteria and determine if results confirm or refute the hypothesis.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Check if LayoutLearner achieves within 20% of profile-guided performance. Compute: (LayoutLearner_F1 / 1.0) >= 0.8. Record: PASS/FAIL
steps.step2: Criterion 2: Check if compilation-time overhead < 5%. Use results from overhead analysis. Record: PASS/FAIL
steps.step3: Criterion 3: Check if profitable transformations identified on >= 40% of benchmarks. Compute: count_correct / total_benchmarks >= 0.4. Record: PASS/FAIL
steps.step4: Criterion 4: Check if LayoutLearner outperforms Static Heuristics baseline. Compute: LayoutLearner_F1 > Heuristics_F1. Record: PASS/FAIL
steps.step5: Overall hypothesis: CONFIRMED if at least 3 of 4 criteria pass, PARTIALLY_CONFIRMED if 2 pass, REFUTED if <=1 passes
steps.step6: Save validation results to data/results/success_criteria.json with detailed breakdown

title: Generate Feature Importance Visualization
description: Create visualization showing which features contribute most to LayoutLearner's predictions.

steps.step1: Extract feature importances from trained XGBoost model using model.feature_importances_
steps.step2: Sort features by importance (gain) and select top 15
steps.step3: Create horizontal bar plot: x-axis=importance score, y-axis=feature name
steps.step4: Color-code by feature category: structural=blue, access pattern=orange, context=green
steps.step5: Add title 'LayoutLearner: Top 15 Feature Importances' and save to figures/feature_importance.pdf (300 DPI)
steps.step6: Also save SHAP summary plot if shap library is available

title: Generate Performance Comparison Plots
description: Create bar charts comparing LayoutLearner against baselines across all metrics.

steps.step1: Create grouped bar chart comparing: LayoutLearner, Static Heuristics, Random Baseline (simulated) across Precision, Recall, F1
steps.step2: Add error bars showing standard deviation across 3 seeds
steps.step3: Add horizontal dashed line at y=0.8 representing 'within 20% of perfect' threshold
steps.step5: Create ablation study bar chart showing performance drop when removing each feature category

title: Generate ROC Curves and Confusion Matrix
description: Create ROC curves for LayoutLearner and baselines, plus confusion matrix for error analysis.

steps.step1: Plot ROC curves for LayoutLearner (mean across 3 seeds) and Static Heuristics
steps.step5: Create confusion matrix heatmap for LayoutLearner predictions (aggregated across all seeds)
steps.step6: Add counts and percentage labels to each cell

steps.step1: Create stacked bar chart showing: baseline time, feature extraction time, inference time per benchmark
steps.step4: Create scatter plot: x-axis=number of struct fields, y-axis=inference time (microseconds)
steps.step5: Add trend line to show scaling behavior

title: Generate Summary Results Table
description: Create LaTeX-formatted table summarizing all key results for paper inclusion.

steps.step1: Create table with rows: LayoutLearner, Static Heuristics, Profile-Guided (upper bound)
steps.step2: Columns: Precision, Recall, F1-Score, Inference Time (us), Overhead (%)
steps.step3: Add ± std for metrics across 3 seeds
steps.step4: Highlight best non-oracle results in bold
steps.step6: Also save as CSV for easy reference: figures/results_table.csv
